rag_rules: Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions; do not reference implementation-only subcomponents or connections
Write AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks in valid component scopes
Declare helper variables for complex expressions to improve readability and maintainability
generic_rules: Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Align helper declaration types with the expression types
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in range expressions
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `guarantee` for property guarantees
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not reference AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not place assumptions or guarantees in invalid scopes for the target component
